question:
Given $n=100$ with 25 successes, and 75 failures, an $80 \%$ two-sided confidence limits on $p$ can be obtained as follows:

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \sum_{i=25}^{100}\binom{100}{i} p_L^i\left(1-p_L\right)^{100-i}=0.10 \\
& \sum_{i=0}^{24}\binom{100}{i} p_U^i\left(1-p_U\right)^{100-i}=0.10
\end{aligned}
$$


Solving the above two equations simultaneously, we obtain

$$
\begin{aligned}
& p_L \approx 0.194 \text { and } p_U \approx 0.313 \\
& P[0.194<p<0.313]=0.80
\end{aligned}
$$
